files.00_Overview.md: Maintain bilingual structure. Add: (1) Learning Objectives section, (2) Prerequisites knowledge required, (3) Detailed file descriptions with estimated reading time, (4) Interactive element showing tool comparison matrix. Keep the concise Quick Reference format.
files.01_ParaView_Visualization.md: Complete 3W Framework: WHAT (ParaView = CFD post-processing standard) is present. Add WHY (when to use vs other tools, advantages for different analysis types). Expand HOW with: (1) Step-by-step annotated screenshots guide, (2) Common pitfalls section, (3) Performance optimization tips for large datasets, (4) Keyboard shortcuts quick reference. Add Learning Objectives and Key Takeaways.
files.02_Python_Plotting.md: Enhance with: (1) Installation/setup instructions for required libraries (fluidfoam, PyVista), (2) Complete working example with sample data structure, (3) Comparison table: matplotlib vs seaborn vs PyVista use cases, (4) Best practices for publication-quality plots, (5) Memory management for large datasets. Add Learning Objectives.
files.03_Blender_Rendering.md: Already excellent with comprehensive 3W and OpenFOAM context. Refine: (1) Add troubleshooting section for common import issues, (2) Include sample render times for different hardware, (3) Add comparison gallery: ParaView vs Blender renders, (4) Provide material preset library (water, fire, smoke) as downloadable appendix, (5) Add workflow decision tree for when to use Blender.
files.04_Animation_Techniques.md: Strong content on technical details. Add: (1) Storyboarding template for animation planning, (2) Frame rate decision guide (30fps vs 60fps use cases), (3) Export format comparison (MP4, AVI, GIF with quality/size tradeoffs), (4) Audio synchronization basics for narrated videos, (5) Complete case study: from raw OpenFOAM data to final animation with all controlDict settings shown.

global_strategy: Create a cohesive visualization learning path progressing from basic ParaView analysis → Python plotting → advanced Blender rendering → professional animation production. Establish consistent pedagogical structure: Learning Objectives → Prerequisites → 3W Framework (What/Why/How) → Step-by-step Tutorials → Common Pitfalls → Key Takeaways → Practice Exercises. Add cross-references between files (e.g., 'For animation basics, see 04_Animation_Techniques.md §3'). Create shared assets: sample dataset links, keyboard shortcut cheatsheet, troubleshooting FAQ appendix. Emphasize practical decision-making: when to use each tool or combination based on project goals (analysis vs presentation).
